seoTitle: How to Add a Watcher to a Jira Ticket
metaDescription: Learn how to add yourself or a teammate as a watcher on a Jira issue so they get notified of updates without being the assignee.
intro: Watching a Jira issue lets you follow its updates without needing to be the assignee or reporter, which is useful for stakeholders who just want visibility. This guide covers adding a watcher to a ticket.

steps.heading: Open the issue
steps.body: Navigate to the Jira ticket you or a teammate wants to follow.

steps.heading: Find the watchers option
steps.body: Look for the watchers icon or link, typically located in the issue's details panel.

steps.heading: Add yourself as a watcher
steps.body: Click to start watching the issue yourself if you want to receive notifications on its updates.

steps.heading: Add someone else as a watcher
steps.body: Use the manage watchers option to search for and add a teammate who should also be notified.

steps.heading: Confirm notifications are working
steps.body: Check that watchers receive updates as expected when the issue changes, such as status updates or new comments.

faq.question: What's the difference between a watcher and an assignee?
faq.answer: An assignee is responsible for completing the work, while a watcher simply receives notifications about updates without owning the task.

faq.question: Can I add someone else as a watcher without their permission?
faq.answer: Yes, if you have the right permissions, you can add a teammate as a watcher directly, though they can remove themselves later if they don't want the notifications.

faq.question: Do watchers get notified about every change to an issue?
faq.answer: Watchers typically receive notifications for major updates like status changes and new comments, based on your Jira notification scheme.

faq.question: Can I stop watching an issue later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can remove yourself from the watchers list at any time if you no longer need updates on that issue.
